why: clear senior backend/infra IC → top for senior-IC
title: Staff+ Infrastructure Engineer, Cluster Infrastructure

facts.title: Staff+ Infrastructure Engineer, Cluster Infrastructure
facts.summary: This Staff Engineer will own the technical strategy for Anthropic's cluster lifecycle management, working with cloud providers and internal teams to scale compute infrastructure. They’ll focus on automation, security, scalability, and operational excellence while mentoring other engineers.
core_requirements: Deep expertise in distributed systems & cloud platforms
Proficiency in a systems language (Rust/Go/Python) + Terraform
Led complex technical initiatives across teams
Ability to align senior stakeholders and communicate effectively

off_growth.rationale: The scale of compute is 'growing faster than at almost any company' implying rapid growth.

off_impact.rationale: Impacts how quickly Anthropic can train models & scale Claude to millions of users.

req_breadth.rationale: Responsibilities span the full lifecycle of compute clusters and collaboration with many teams.

off_autonomy.rationale: Owns technical strategy and roadmap; aligns with multiple teams implies significant autonomy.

off_building.rationale: Owns strategy & roadmap for cluster lifecycle management; builds automation systems.

req_seniority.rationale: Staff Engineer title and 'setting technical direction' imply high seniority.

off_comp_level.rationale: £325k-£485k is competitive for a Staff Engineer in the UK.

req_leadership.rationale: Mentorship mentioned but no direct reports specified; leadership is a supporting function.

off_company_risk.rationale: Anthropic is well funded and growing but still relatively early stage; not an established enterprise.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: AI infrastructure is a hot field but not an unusual career step for experienced engineers.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires 'deep expertise' in multiple complex areas (distributed systems, cloud platforms).

off_people_leadership.rationale: Mentorship is mentioned but no team leadership responsibilities are explicit.

facts.title: Engineering Manager, Marketplace
facts.summary: This Engineering Manager will build and lead a team to develop the Marketplace Platform at Anthropic, focusing on connecting customers with partners. They'll own the entire engineering lifecycle, from hiring to incident response, while coordinating closely with other platform teams.

off_growth.rationale: The team is 'quickly growing' and the role involves building something new – high growth potential.

off_impact.rationale: The Marketplace Platform is described as a key product surface with integration across the Claude ecosystem.

req_breadth.rationale: The JD emphasizes coordination across multiple teams (billing, auth) and working with customers/partners – a broad scope.

off_autonomy.rationale: JD states 'broad mandate' and ownership of execution end-to-end.

off_building.rationale: Described as a '0→1' role building a new product layer; implies significant hands-on creation.

req_seniority.rationale: The role involves forming a team and setting technical direction, indicating significant experience is needed.

off_comp_level.rationale: $405k-$485k for an EM role in San Francisco is competitive but not *leading* edge.

req_leadership.rationale: Responsibilities explicitly include hiring, managing, and growing engineers; direct reports are central to the job.

off_company_risk.rationale: Anthropic is a well-funded AI company; relatively low risk compared to seed-stage startups.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Building a marketplace platform isn’t *radically* different from other EM roles, but AI-specific context adds some novelty.

req_technical_depth.rationale: The role requires understanding 'seams' with other platform teams and driving platform readiness, implying technical expertise.

off_people_leadership.rationale: The core of the job is forming and leading a team ('hire the founding engineers…build a healthy team').

why: clear enterprise AE → top for sales
title: Commercial Account Executive - Mid-Market, US West

facts.title: Commercial Account Executive - Mid-Market, US West
facts.summary: This Commercial Account Executive role focuses on managing a book of mid-market business (250-1999 employees) within the US, driving sales of GitLab's DevSecOps platform through value-based selling and collaboration with various internal teams. They’ll be responsible for prospecting, closing deals, ensuring customer adoption, and reducing churn.

off_growth.rationale: The JD highlights GitLab's growth and the opportunity to accelerate careers.

off_impact.rationale: Impact on revenue goals is clear, but within an established company.

req_breadth.rationale: Needs to understand both business & technical needs of customers; broad scope across the sales cycle.

off_autonomy.rationale: Owns a book of business and manages the sales cycle with some guidance from teams.

off_building.rationale: Focus is on selling an existing platform, not building new products.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires experience selling SaaS and managing accounts, implying mid-to-senior level.

off_comp_level.rationale: Salary range ($68,800 - $114,600) is competitive for the role and location.

req_leadership.rationale: JD focuses on individual account ownership; no mention of team management.

off_company_risk.rationale: GitLab is a well-established company with significant market share.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: DevSecOps is evolving but a fairly established domain; not a radical pivot.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires understanding DevSecOps and technical stakeholders, but not deep engineering expertise.

off_people_leadership.rationale: Role is individual contributor; collaboration mentioned but no direct reports.

title: Senior B2B Marketing Manager, DET (UK, Europe & ANZ)

facts.title: Senior B2B Marketing Manager, DET (UK, Europe & ANZ)
facts.summary: This role focuses on scaling the Duolingo English Test in the UK and ANZ through B2B marketing. The person will lead regional strategy, execute events/partnerships to drive adoption among universities and governments, and build scalable systems for long-term growth.
core_requirements: Ability to build processes in ambiguity

off_growth.rationale: Scaling into new markets suggests moderate growth potential for the role.

off_impact.rationale: Impact on adoption/engagement metrics within a growing product line.

req_breadth.rationale: Needs understanding of education landscape and cross-functional partnership.

off_autonomy.rationale: Role is described as independent decision-making and ownership of strategy.

off_building.rationale: Building 'scalable systems' suggests some creation, but primarily scaling existing programs.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires 7-10+ years of experience and leading regional strategy.

off_comp_level.rationale: Salary range is competitive for the UK market (£90k-£123k).

req_leadership.rationale: Focus is on execution; no mention of direct reports or team management.

off_company_risk.rationale: Duolingo is a well-established and growing company.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: EdTech marketing is a specialized field but not an extreme domain pivot for experienced marketers.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires marketing skills but doesn't specify deep technical expertise.

off_people_leadership.rationale: No mention of managing a team; focuses on cross-functional partnership.

title: Staff Data Analyst

facts.title: Staff Data Analyst
facts.summary: This role focuses on defining and implementing the data strategy for Stripe's risk-as-a-product offering. The person will build metrics, pipelines, and analytical frameworks to support risk decisioning, experimentation, and reporting while also mentoring other analysts.
core_requirements: Experience defining data strategy across teams
Deep expertise in SQL and Python

off_growth.rationale: Building a new 'risk as a product' offering suggests significant growth and change for the role.

off_impact.rationale: Shaping risk capabilities as a product has high potential impact on Stripe's business.

req_breadth.rationale: Role spans strategy, experimentation, and productization of risk data – a broad scope.

off_autonomy.rationale: Defining data strategy and owning metrics suggests significant autonomy in approach.

off_building.rationale: Building 'data products' implies some hands-on creation, but also leveraging existing infrastructure.

req_seniority.rationale: 10+ years experience and shaping data strategy implies a very senior role.

off_comp_level.rationale: Stripe is known for competitive compensation; no stated range implies it will be market-aligned or above.

req_leadership.rationale: Mentoring analysts and influencing teams suggests moderate leadership responsibilities.

off_company_risk.rationale: Stripe is a well-established, high-growth company – relatively low risk.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: While data analytics is common, applying it to risk-as-a-product in fintech represents some domain novelty.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Deep expertise in SQL/Python + building data products indicates high technical depth.

off_people_leadership.rationale: Mentoring is present, but the description doesn’t suggest direct reports or team management.

title: Staff Engineer, Datacenter Server Lifecycle

facts.title: Staff Engineer, Datacenter Server Lifecycle
facts.summary: This role focuses on the full lifecycle of datacenter servers – from initial setup and operation to maintenance and decommissioning. The engineer will define processes, automation, and standards for hardware management at scale, with a strong emphasis on security and trusted compute.
core_requirements: Proficiency in a programming language (Python, Rust, Go, Java)
Knowledge of cloud infrastructure (Kubernetes, IaC, AWS/GCP)

off_growth.rationale: The company is expanding beyond cloud infrastructure.

off_impact.rationale: Impacts all machines in the facility handling sensitive AI workloads.

req_breadth.rationale: Requires understanding of the entire lifecycle *and* integration with networking/security teams.

off_autonomy.rationale: The engineer will 'own' the server lifecycle and define standards.

off_building.rationale: The role is 'greenfield work' and involves building tooling from scratch.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires defining strategy and owning end-to-end processes.

off_comp_level.rationale: £325k-£390k is a competitive salary for this type of role in London.

req_leadership.rationale: JD focuses on individual ownership; no mention of direct reports.

off_company_risk.rationale: Anthropic is growing but established; not a seed startup.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Focus on trusted compute and AI hardware lifecycle is specialized.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Deep expertise in server hardware, security, and cloud infrastructure is essential.

off_people_leadership.rationale: No mention of managing a team; focus on individual ownership.

facts.summary: This leader will build and manage a team of Solutions Architects at Stripe, focusing on growing existing customer accounts through upselling and cross-selling. They'll collaborate with sales, product, and engineering teams to drive revenue retention and ensure customer success by aligning technical solutions with business needs.

off_growth.rationale: Leading a growing team within a rapidly scaling business suggests significant role evolution.

off_impact.rationale: Impacts revenue retention and growth through upsells/cross-sells with large customers.

req_breadth.rationale: Needs to work across multiple teams (sales, marketing, product) but is focused on pre-sales.

off_autonomy.rationale: Leads strategy & execution for the segment; empowered to drive results.

off_building.rationale: Focuses on applying existing Stripe solutions, not building new products.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires 16+ years of technical sales experience and leadership.

off_comp_level.rationale: Stripe is a well-funded growth company; likely competitive compensation.

req_leadership.rationale: Explicitly responsible for building, leading, retaining, and coaching a team.

off_company_risk.rationale: Stripe is an established and successful fintech company.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Pre-sales leadership in fintech is a logical progression for experienced sales professionals.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires deep understanding of APIs, payments, and technical sales.

off_people_leadership.rationale: Core responsibility is leading and growing a team of solution architects.

title: Media Engineer, Social

facts.title: Media Engineer, Social

facts.location: Hybrid - San Francisco, New York City
core_requirements: Experience with video, threads, and visuals
Familiarity with AI tools for content creation

off_growth.rationale: JD mentions Vercel's fast growth and the need to 'grow' social presence.

off_impact.rationale: Shaping how developers discover Vercel suggests significant impact on brand perception.

off_building.rationale: Building 'simple systems or scripts' is a component, but not the primary focus.

req_leadership.rationale: JD focuses on individual execution; no mention of managing a team.

off_company_risk.rationale: Vercel is a well-funded growth stage company; moderate risk.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Social media is a standard function, but applying it specifically to developer audiences adds some novelty.

facts.title: Staff+ Security Engineer, Risk Engineering
facts.summary: This role focuses on building and owning Anthropic's security risk management systems. The person will identify, assess, and drive remediation of complex security risks, working closely with Security Engineering to define the program and build AI-native platforms for scalable risk assessment.
facts.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY | Seattle, WA
core_requirements: Strong programming skills (Python, Go, Rust, C++)
Broad knowledge of core security domains

off_growth.rationale: The role involves 'greenfield work' and defining new systems, suggesting fast growth potential.

off_impact.rationale: Direct line to CISO decisions and shaping the security program implies high impact.

req_breadth.rationale: Needs breadth across the entire security landscape (identity, infrastructure, etc.).

off_autonomy.rationale: Expects ownership of complex problems with 'minimal oversight'.

off_building.rationale: Focuses on 'building' risk management systems and AI-native platforms.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires 8+ years experience and leading complex risks independently.

off_comp_level.rationale: $320k-$405k is competitive for this type of role in San Francisco.

req_leadership.rationale: Mentors engineers but no explicit team management is stated.

off_company_risk.rationale: Anthropic is a growing company but established and well-funded.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Building AI-native risk management is a relatively novel domain.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires deep understanding of security systems and ability to 'go deep' in any domain.

off_people_leadership.rationale: Mentoring is mentioned, but no direct reports are implied.

facts.title: Senior Solutions Engineer - West

They’ll conduct technical discovery, design solutions, and influence product direction based on customer needs. The role involves presenting demos, leading workshops, and proactively communicating with stakeholders.
facts.location: CA Remote (BC & ON only); U.S. Remote

off_growth.rationale: The role emphasizes staying curious and embracing emerging technologies like AI, suggesting opportunities for learning.

off_impact.rationale: Influences product direction based on customer feedback but doesn't own a company-wide metric.

req_breadth.rationale: The role requires cross-functional collaboration with Sales, Customer Success, and Product teams.

off_autonomy.rationale: The role involves proactive communication and independent solution delivery within customer engagements.

off_building.rationale: Focuses on demonstrating existing platform capabilities rather than building new features.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires 3-5+ years of experience as a Solutions Engineer and expects to 'guide customers' through complex evaluations.

off_comp_level.rationale: Salary range ($156,500 - $210,000 USD) is competitive for the experience level and location.

req_leadership.rationale: The role focuses on individual customer engagements; no team management is mentioned.

off_company_risk.rationale: Webflow appears to be a growth-stage company but established enough to not be high risk.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Solutions Engineering is a standard next step for experienced technical professionals.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires understanding of APIs and web programming languages (HTML, CSS, JavaScript) for solution design.

off_people_leadership.rationale: Reports to a manager and partners with Account Executives; no direct reports mentioned.

facts.title: Partner Development Manager- SaaS Platforms
facts.summary: This role focuses on advising strategic SaaS platform partners in the EMEA region to drive growth and adoption of Stripe's products. The PDM will develop and validate regional growth tactics, then scale successful initiatives across the broader Stripe Partner Ecosystem. They act as a consultant, helping platforms acquire customers and expand their use of embedded financial products.

off_growth.rationale: Early EMEA hire suggests some degree of organizational building/scaling within the region.

off_impact.rationale: Impact is on program-level outcomes (scaling tactics) and platform growth, not the entire company.

req_breadth.rationale: Role spans strategy, growth tactics, and cross-functional collaboration – broader than a single system.

off_autonomy.rationale: Strategic advisor role implies significant ownership over partner relationships and growth plans.

off_building.rationale: Focus is on advising and scaling existing solutions, not building new ones from scratch.

req_seniority.rationale: 8+ years experience and strategic advisory role suggest a senior level.

off_comp_level.rationale: No compensation information provided; assuming competitive for a role at Stripe.

req_leadership.rationale: JD focuses on individual contribution as an advisor; no mention of direct reports.

off_company_risk.rationale: Stripe is an established and well-funded company with low risk of failure.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: SaaS platform partnerships is a common domain; not a radical pivot for most candidates.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires understanding of platforms and products but isn't a deeply technical role.

off_people_leadership.rationale: No mention of managing a team; role is advisory and individual contributor focused.

facts.title: Product Manager, Developer Productivity
facts.summary: This Product Manager will define and own the strategy for Anthropic's developer platform, focusing on build systems, CI/CD, and AI-assisted tooling. They’ll partner with engineering teams to improve developer velocity and ensure a scalable infrastructure as AI agents become more involved in the software development process.
facts.location: San Francisco, CA | New York City, NY
core_requirements: 7+ years product management experience in developer tooling/platform infrastructure
Experience scaling platform products from infancy to serve demanding customers
Ability to balance velocity, reliability, and security trade-offs
Deep understanding of build systems, CI pipelines, and accelerator toolchains
Strong thesis on how AI will reshape software development

off_growth.rationale: Rapidly scaling engineering organization; defining the future of AI-assisted development.

off_impact.rationale: Directly impacts engineering velocity across the entire company.

req_breadth.rationale: Needs to balance needs across multiple engineering personas.

off_autonomy.rationale: Owns strategy and roadmap; drives product vision.

off_building.rationale: Focus on defining and owning the developer platform implies significant building.

req_seniority.rationale: Requires 7+ years experience and building/scaling products.

off_comp_level.rationale: $385k-$595k is competitive for a PM role in San Francisco.

req_leadership.rationale: Focus is on cross-functional partnership, not direct reports.

off_company_risk.rationale: Anthropic is well-funded and growing, but still relatively early stage.

off_domain_novelty.rationale: Focus on AI-native developer tooling is a relatively new domain.

req_technical_depth.rationale: Requires deep understanding of complex technical systems (build systems, monorepos).

off_people_leadership.rationale: JD emphasizes cross-functional partnership, not team management.
